items.title: Stacking linear layers in a neural network
items.scenario: You have an input batch X of shape (batch_size x din), a first fully connected layer with weight matrix W1 of shape (din x h), and a second fully connected layer with weight matrix W2 of shape (h x dout).
items.explanation: The first layer output is H = XW1 with shape (batch_size x h), and the final output is Y = HW2 = X(W1W2) with shape (batch_size x dout). Instead of applying W1 and W2 separately at inference time, you can precompute the combined weight matrix Wcombined = W1W2 and use Y = XWcombined, which works because matrix multiplication composes the two linear maps into one.

items.title: 2D rotation followed by scaling
items.scenario: In 2D graphics you want to rotate points by 45 degrees and then scale them by a factor of 2 in both x and y directions.
items.explanation: Let R be the 2 x 2 rotation matrix and S be the 2 x 2 scaling matrix with 2 on the diagonal. The overall transformation is SR. For any point x in R2, SRx is the same as first computing y = Rx (rotation) and then z = Sy (scaling), but implemented as a single matrix multiply per point.

items.title: Adjacency matrices and paths in a graph
items.scenario: You represent a directed graph with n nodes using an adjacency matrix A, where Ai,j is 1 if there is an edge from node i to node j and 0 otherwise.
items.explanation: The square A2 = AA is an n x n matrix where entry A2i,j counts the number of distinct paths of length 2 from i to j. More generally, Ak encodes information about paths of length k. This works because each entry in A2 sums products over all intermediate nodes, mirroring the combinatorics of two-step walks in the graph.

items.title: Propagating probabilities in a Markov chain
items.scenario: You model a Markov chain with n states using a stochastic transition matrix P where Pi,j is the probability of moving from state i to state j in one step.
items.explanation: If you represent the current state distribution as a row vector q of length n, then after one step the new distribution is qP. After k steps it is qPk. Powers of the matrix P are computed with matrix multiplication and let you predict multi-step behaviour of the chain.
